(iii) “Technical” Posts: Joint Director [Extension], Deputy Director [Extension] and Extension Inspector, except as respects things done or omitted to be done before such supersession, the Central Government hereby makes the following rules, namely:- 1. SHORT TITLE AND COMMENCEMENT. (a) These rules may be called the Coffee Board (Cadre and Recruitment) Rules, 2014. (b) They shall come into force on the date of its publication in the Official Gazette. 2. Definitions. In these rules, unless the context otherwise requires, (a) “Cadre” refers to classification of posts and the sanctioned strength of posts in each cadre from the notification of these rules and is as listed in Annexure – I to these rules; (b) “Competent Authority” means the Chairman of the Board; (c) ‘Executive Committee’ means the Committee constituted as Executive Committee under the Coffee Act, 1942 as amended from time to time. (d) “Government” means the Central Government. (e) “Schedule” means the Schedule annexed to these rules. -- 50 of 102 -- [*TPTII-W3(i)] TO^W:WqW i i (f) “Scientific”, “Technical” and “Non-Technical” - Administrative or Ministerial Posts means the posts classified as such in the Annexure - I, to these rules. 3. Recruitment. The method of recruitment, qualifications, experience, age and other requirements shall be as mentioned in Schedule to these rules. 4. Relaxation in Age limit and Experience. (a) The minimum Age Limit for entry into the Coffee Board is as prescribed by the Government of India from time to time. (b) The upper or maximum Age Limit for appointment to various posts is as per the guidelines of the Government of India, Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances and Pensions, Department of Personnel and Training, vide Office Memorandum No. AB.14017/48/2010-Estt.(RR) dated 31st December, 2010 as amended from time to time. (c) The Upper age limit is relaxable in the case of Scheduled Castes or Scheduled Tribes or Other Backward Classes or Persons with Disabilities or Ex-Servicemen candidates as may be prescribed by the Government of India from time to time. (d) Wherever posts are meant for direct recruitment, in-service Personnel possessing requisite qualification and experience can also apply. (e) The upper age limit for in-service or departmental candidates for appointment by direct recruitment to Group ‘C’ posts is up to forty years in accordance with the instructions or orders issued by the Government of India from time to time. (f) The upper age-limit for in-service or departmental candidates for appointment by direct recruitment to Group ‘A’ and ‘B’ posts is relaxable up to five years in accordance with the instructions or orders issued by the Government of India from time to time. (g) In case of promotion the requirement of experience may be relaxed up to three years in the case of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribe employees or in accordance with the instructions or orders issued by the Government of India from time to time. (h) The Research Personnel viz., Research Associates, Senior Research Fellow (SRF), Junior Research Fellow (JRF), Technical Assistants etc., engaged on Contract or Ad-hoc basis in Coffee Board under various Research Programme, are eligible for relaxation of upper age-limit to the extent of the period of service rendered by them on Contract or Ad-hoc basis in Coffee Board subject to a maximum of five years for appointment to Group ‘A’ and ‘B’ posts, and to a maximum of upper age-limit of forty years in case of appointment to Group ‘C’ posts, on par with the in-service or departmental Personnel. (i) The Permanent Mazdoors working in Research Farms of the Coffee Board are eligible for relaxation of upper age- limit to the extent of the period of service rendered by them as Permanent Mazdoors in Coffee Board subject to a maximum of upper age limit of forty years in case of appointment to Group ‘C’ posts, on par with the in-service or departmental Personnel. (j) The requirement regarding experience is relaxable at the discretion of the competent authority in the case of Scheduled Caste or Scheduled Tribe candidates, if at any stage of selection, the Competent Authority is of the opinion that sufficient numbers of candidates from these communities possessing the requisite experience are not likely to be available to fill up the vacancies reserved for them and the appointing authority shall also record the reasons for relaxing the experience in writing while doing so. 5. Appointing Authority. Subject to the provisions of the Coffee Act, 1942, the appointing authority shall be as under: (a) Government of India, in the case of Chairman, Secretary and Director of Finance. (b) Chairman, with the approval of the Government, in the case of Director of Research and other posts identified as key posts by the Government of India. -- 51 of 102 -- 52 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] (c) Chairman, subject to selection by Executive Committee, in the case of Joint Director [Research], Joint Director [Extension], Divisional Heads, Divisional Head [Engineering], Deputy Director [Research], Deputy Director [Extension], Deputy Director [Administration or Accounts] and Deputy Director [Official Language]. (d) Chairman, subject to selection by the Departmental Promotion Committee [DPC] or the Recruitment Committee [RC] in respect of the posts carrying Grade Pay of `4600 to `5400. (e) Secretary, subject to selection by the Departmental Promotion Committee [DPC] or the Recruitment Committee [RC] and with the approval of Chairman, in respect of the posts carrying Grade Pay up to `4200. (f) Any other officer of the Board, subject to selection by the Departmental Promotion Committee [DPC] or the Recruitment Committee [RC], in respect of such posts for which he or she is specifically empowered by the competent authority. 6. Mode of Recruitment. I. Direct Recruitment. (a) For Group ‘A’ and ‘B’ Posts. Direct recruitment shall always be through advertisement only; provided that, for reasons to be recorded in writing, the Chairman is competent to fill a post, through Employment Exchange. (b) For Group ‘C’ Posts. Wherever vacancies are to be filled it shall be through Advertisement or Staff Selection Commission or Employment Exchange. If necessary, Direct Recruitment to the posts of Extension Inspector, Junior Clerk, Driver - Ordinary Grade and Multi-Tasking Staff may be made at Regional or State Levels. Wherever posts are meant for direct recruitment, in-service Personnel possessing requisite qualification and experience can also apply. Age-limit can be relaxed in such cases, if otherwise the in-service personnel are found suitable. The Selection Committee may prepare a select list of candidates found suitable which shall remain valid for a period of twelve months in the normal course and in exceptional cases the appointing authority may extend the validity of the select panel by another six months. An appointment shall take effect from the date on which the appointee joins duty in the Coffee Board. The method of promotion shall be as specified in Schedule to these rules. The composition of the Departmental Promotion Committee and the Recruitment Committee shall be as notified by the Chairman in accordance as far as possible with the rules applicable to Government employees of equivalent cadres. In-service Personnel are required to pass departmental competitive examination or departmental tests wherever prescribed separately for becoming eligible to next higher posts provided they possess requisite qualification and experience. The minimum qualifying service for promotion from one grade to another shall be as per the guidelines of Department of Personnel and Training, issued vide O.M. No. AB.14017/61/2008-Estt. (RR) dated the 24th March, 2009 as amended from time to time. Promotion shall be according to the ratio or quota as prescribed wherever applicable. Where quota is not specified, the promotion shall be considered from the Common Gradation List of feeder cadres. (i) (ii) (c) (d) (e) II. The period of probation for Direct Recruitment and Promotion in Group ‘A’, ‘B’ and ‘C’ shall be two years as per the general principles laid down by the Government of India from time to time. 8. Reservation.The reservation for Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Other Backward Classes, Persons with Disabilities, Ex-Serviceman and other categories of candidates shall be followed strictly in accordance with the instructions of the Government of India, issued from time to time. 9. Pay Scale. If there is to be any change in the Pay Scale, Pay Band or Grade Pay, it will be deemed to have been effected in the Recruitment Rules against such posts from time to time. -- 53 of 102 -- 54 THE GAZETTE OF INDIA : EXTRAORDINARY [PART II—SEC. 3(i)] 10. Modified Assured Career Progression Scheme [MACPS]. The Scheme of Modified Assured Career Progression Scheme [MACPS] approved vide Department of Personnel and Training O.M. No. 35034/3/2008-Estt. (D) Dated the 19th May, 2009 shall be applicable and followed strictly in accordance with the instructions contained therein and as amended from time to time. 11. Modified Flexible Complementing Scheme [MFCS]. The Scheme of Modified Flexible Complementing Scheme [MFCS], approved vide Department of Personnel and Training O.M. No. AB-14017/37/2008-Estt. (RR) dated the 10th September, 2010 shall be applicable and followed strictly in accordance with the instructions contained therein and as amended from time to time. 12. Other Schemes. If necessary, the Board may implement schemes for Career Improvement of its employees with the approval of the Government of India or Ministry of Commerce and Industry. 13. Age-Limits. The maximum and minimum age-limits mentioned against the direct recruitment posts shall be calculated on the last date for receipt of applications. 14. Transfer Liability. All employees shall be liable for transfer or posting anywhere in India. 15. Residuary Matters. In respect of all matters not specifically provided for in these rules, the corresponding provisions prescribed by the Government of India relating to its employees, as amended from time to time, will be followed. 16. Interpretation.
